sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 31900270 with Dell Marketing LP (“Dell”) for the purchase of hardware, software, and
related services utilizing the County of Riverside Agreement 8084445, for an additional
$2,786,057 for a total not to exceed value of $8,197,733 for the period commencing on
November 1, 2019 and through October 31, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Council authority is requested to allow for continued hardware and software purchases
from Dell under County of Riverside Agreement 8084445, a competitively bid cooperative
purchasing agreement, to support citywide enterprise computing equipment (PC’s,
laptops, servers, storage, network equipment, software, etc.). Funding is available
through a combination of the Department of Information Technology’s Fiscal Year (FY)
2026 IT Cost Allocation Fund and various City Departments on an as-procured basis, as
City Departments fund their cost for procured equipment.
sections.current_situation:
The City of Berkeley participates in a competitively bid cooperative pricing agreement
negotiated by the County of Riverside (Agreement No. 8084445) with Dell to obtain
hardware, software, and related services. This agreement provides the City of Berkeley
with standardized hardware and software at competitive prices and facilitates a consistent
infrastructure and computing experience, as well as efficient troubleshooting and quick
turnaround times for equipment replacement.
This agreement aligns with the City’s strategic priority to provide “state-of-the-art, well-
maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities”.
Internal
Page 2 of 3
sections.background:
Formally, the City utilized a cooperative purchasing agreement known as WSCA, a
cooperative purchasing arm of NASPO (a conglomeration of purchasing directors from
fifteen western states formed to leverage their combined purchasing power to achieve
competitive pricing discounts and streamline the acquisition of quality products and
services from industry-leading vendors). The WSCA NASPO cooperative agreement
terminated effective July 31, 2023 and no extension is available.
As a result, the City changed its cooperative agreement to use the County of Riverside
Agreement. This competitively bid, cooperative agreement is used by multiple cities and
counties in the State of California, including the State itself. Leveraging this cooperative
agreement provides the City the best pricing vs. all competitive bids and cost models.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The technology equipment purchased from Dell is energy efficient and meets the
standards required by law. The purchase and subsequent disposal of equipment is done
responsibly using e-waste vendors and community non-profit agencies that provide
technology equipment back into local communities to address the digital divide.
Additionally, keeping the City’s inventory routinely updated allows the City to purchase
the most energy efficient hardware to meet the City’s goal of reducing energy
consumption and maintaining an environmentally sound information technology
infrastructure.
sections.rationale:
Using competitively bid, cooperative purchasing agreements allows the City to gain
greater efficiencies and economies of scale. The County of Riverside Agreement
8084445 is used by multiple cities and counties in the State of California and including
the State itself.
Dell continues to provide excellent hardware and software to support the City’s
enterprise computing needs, including network infrastructure, servers, and desktop
computers. Dell also continues to provide exemplary customer service and response
times that contributes to overall employee productivity and addresses the cybersecurity
requirements of the City.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered issuing a specific bid for the City of Berkeley’s computer hardware and
software requirements, but at this time, doing so would not yield better pricing than that
which is currently established through the County of Riverside Agreement 8084445.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology Department 510-981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Internal
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
DELL MARKETING LP (“DELL”): USING COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE AGREEMENT
8084445 FOR HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE PURCHASES
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley relies upon a technically sound and efficient computer
network; and
WHEREAS, by purchasing from competitively bid, cooperative purchasing agreements
such as County of Riverside to Agreement 8084445, the City of Berkeley is able to take
advantage of pre-negotiated prices, economies of scale, and increased efficiencies; and
WHEREAS, hardware and software for various City-wide enterprise and infrastructure
projects is purchased from Dell, and maximizes the value of the City’s investment by
adopting a citywide standard for equipment, such as PC’s, laptops and servers; and
WHEREAS, Dell continues to offer the best overall value, reliable hardware, and
excellent customer service since 1994; and
WHEREAS, enterprise funding is provided by Department of Information Technology’s
Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 IT Cost Allocation Fund and various city department funds on an
as-procured basis, as city departments fund their cost for procured equipment;
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to increase the spending authority for purchase orders for
computer hardware, software, and related services from Dell utilizing pricing from the
County of Riverside Agreement 8084445 by an additional amount of $2,786,057 for a
total not to exceed value of $8,197,733 for the period commencing on November 1, 2019
and through October 31, 2027.
